validation_sources: Reddit discussions on on-device LLM challenges
Hacker News threads on edge AI deployment
GitHub issues on llama.cpp, ExecuTorch, LiteRT, MediaPipe
Academic papers on edge LLM inference
Industry blogs and surveys on edge AI deployment

demand_signals.signal: Open issue asking for steps to convert models to .litertlm format for Qualcomm NPU — developers struggling with format-specific NPU targeting
Hardware heterogeneity identified as one of the top deployment challenges — different NPUs require different optimization paths
People over-index on TOPS, but decode-time inference is memory-bandwidth bound — NPU utilization varies wildly across vendors
Samsung's toolchain is entirely separate from Qualcomm and Apple, forcing triple development effort
RAG on Mobile: Local Vector DBs and Smart Search 2026 — entire article addressing this unmet need
Thread asking 'What is the best architecture for integrating local LLM inference and RAG on mobile devices?' — no clear answer
Open-source project specifically for RAG pipeline on Android — proves demand exists but solution is immature
Vector database vendor specifically targeting on-device RAG use case — market forming
Article on implementing SLMs with RAG on embedded devices for cost reduction, privacy, and offline use
1000+ Android device SKUs with wildly different memory, NPU, and thermal characteristics — manual optimization is unsustainable
llama-bench bugs with incorrect backend defaults show how manual device profiling fails at scale
Paper on 'Edge-First Language Model Inference: Models, Metrics, and Tradeoffs' — highlights lack of systematic approach to model-device matching
Paper on 'LLM Inference at the Edge: Mobile, NPU, and GPU Performance Efficiency Trade-offs Under Sustained Load' shows massive perf variance across devices
Safety-critical deployments (automotive via TensorRT-Edge-LLM, medical devices) lack any reasoning introspection tools
Cloud LLM observability is booming (Langfuse, LangSmith, Datadog) but ZERO tools target on-device inference specifically
Automotive and robotics identified as primary edge LLM adopters — debugging is critical for certification

validated_gaps.title: Cross-Platform Mobile LLM SDK (React Native / Flutter / KMP)

demand_signals.signal: Cactus v1 launch coverage highlights React Native/Flutter/KMP support as KEY differentiator — proves market demand
Tutorial 'How to run private, on-device AI in your Flutter App using Cactus' — developer hunger for cross-platform mobile LLM SDK
'LLM Inference on Edge: A Fun and Easy Guide to run LLMs via React Native on your Phone!' — community-driven effort showing demand
React Native + Flutter represent >60% of mobile app development — massive underserved segment for on-device LLM

validated_gaps.title: On-Device Model Fine-Tuning / Personalization

demand_signals.signal: QVAC Fabric LLM launched as 'first production-ready framework for LLM training on smartphone-class hardware' — proves market gap is being addressed
Academic framework for end-to-end LLM fine-tuning on mobile phones — research-stage, not production
Privacy-Aware and Efficient LLM Fine-Tuning on Mobile — multiple research groups tackling this
EdgeLoRA for efficient multi-tenant LoRA serving on edge — academic solution for shared model personalization
On-device personalization via local fine-tuning identified as key 2026 trend — 'could deliver user-specific behavior without shipping private data off-device'

validated_gaps.evidence_quality: very strong (multiple research papers + commercial product)

demand_signals.signal: CI/CD pipelines, staged rollouts, telemetry hooks, and auto-healing for edge fleet — described as needed but not available as turnkey platform
OTA model updates highlighted as a key selling point — validates market demand
Asynchronous OTA updates identified as critical for edge LLM deployment at scale
Comprehensive survey on 'Collaborative Inference and Learning between Edge SLMs and Cloud LLMs' — entire research subfield forming
'Hybrid SLM and LLM for Edge-Cloud Collaborative Inference' — academic validation of the concept
AWS blog on on-device SLMs with agentic orchestration for telecom — describes routing queries between edge and cloud
'The Future of LLM Routing: On-device, Edge AI, and Federated Models' — industry recognition of gap
Token-level edge-cloud collaboration achieving significant cost efficiency — research maturing
Reasoning distillation from frontier models to SLMs identified as key 2026 trend
'Edge AI: Why Small Language Models Win for Privacy, Speed, and Cost' — but no toolkit to create domain-specific reasoning SLMs
Academic paper on 'Small Language Models: Architecture, Evolution, and the Future' — covers distillation but no turnkey tools
On-device SLMs with agentic orchestration — AWS describing the pattern for telecom
Engineering a Local-First Agentic Podcast Studio with Multi-Agent Orchestration — proves demand for local multi-agent
'Toward Edge General Intelligence with Multiple-Large Language Model (Multi-LLM): Architecture, Trust, and Orchestration' — academic foundation forming
Popular agent frameworks are all cloud-centric — on-device gap is clear
Paper proposing 'novel benchmarking framework and metric' for LLM inference on edge — proves lack of standard
Edge-Device Large Language Model Competition — academic community creating ad-hoc benchmarks
Discussion 'Benchmarking: What types of performance does LLMs care about?' — community confused about what to measure
LLM Inference Benchmarking Cheat Sheet exists but covers hardware, not edge-specific scenarios
Gemini Nano deployed on Pixel Watch via LiteRT-LM — Google validating the wearable LLM use case
Claims wearable support as differentiator — market sees demand
50KB footprint targets MCUs — but no turnkey wearable SDK
Healthcare and finance repeatedly cited as key on-device LLM use cases, but no framework has HIPAA/SOC2 certification
Privacy-Aware fine-tuning research shows academic concern about formal privacy guarantees
Apple's Private Cloud Compute is the closest to formal privacy guarantees but only for Apple ecosystem

validated_gaps.title: On-Device Structured Output / JSON Mode Guarantees

demand_signals.signal: Fast JSON Decoding for Local LLMs with Compressed FSM — proving demand for constrained decoding locally
Libraries exist for constrained decoding but NOT integrated into mobile runtimes (ExecuTorch, LiteRT, etc.)
Server-side structured output mature, but zero mobile/edge implementations
GitHub project for low-latency JSON from LLMs — references MLX but no mobile support
9+ cloud LLM observability tools exist (Langfuse, LangSmith, Datadog, etc.) — NONE target on-device inference
Telemetry hooks and logging agents described as needed for edge AI fleet management
LLM observability is a rapidly growing category — edge is the obvious next frontier
Offline operation repeatedly cited as a key benefit of on-device LLMs, but no SDK optimizes for this end-to-end
Defense and field service consistently mentioned as target segments for offline AI
Blog on SLMs with RAG on embedded devices specifically for offline use — proves demand

validated_gaps.title: Cross-Device Model Sharing & Federated Edge Inference

demand_signals.signal: Distributed Split Speculative Decoding for collaborative edge-device inference — research forming
Paper on enabling LLM inference via partitioning across edge devices
Federated LoRA for lightweight fine-tuning across devices without centralizing data
Issue asking 'Steps to Convert A LLM model to litertlm format for Qualcomm NPU' — conversion is painful
Issues with model conversion producing broken files (falcon, phi-2, stablelm) — conversion errors common
Tutorial on edge LLM deployment via React Native highlights conversion complexity as a major barrier
GGUF, ONNX, Core ML, .pte, TensorRT, QNN, Exynos compiled — 7+ formats with no unified converter
TensorRT-Edge-LLM launched specifically for automotive/robotics — validates the segment
Bosch AI Cockpit uses TensorRT-Edge-LLM — real production demand
Hailo-10H targeting edge LLM for automotive — multiple hardware vendors entering

validated_gaps.title: Free/Open Model Marketplace with Edge-Optimized Artifacts

demand_signals.signal: Massive model hub but few edge-optimized artifacts with per-device benchmarks
Model library is popular but only GGUF — no ExecuTorch, LiteRT, QNN artifacts
Google launched AI Edge Gallery on Play Store — validates demand for curated edge models
